fields.label: Human-readable node label
fields.kind: listed_company | private_company | fund | state_agency | nominee | custodian_bank | person | educational_institution
fields.symbol: SET ticker when the node is a listed Thai company
fields.market: Primary market for listed companies, e.g. SET
fields.displayShape: circle for listed companies, rect for non-listed entities
fields.tags: Optional string tags used for filtering

fields.pctVoting: Voting-right percentage when different from shareholding
fields.asOfDate: YYYY-MM-DD date attached to the reported ownership snapshot
fields.sourceRefs: List of source ids from the top-level sources array
fields.notes: Short explanation for context or caveats

sources.label: GPSC listed company snapshot major shareholders
CPN major shareholders and shareholder structure

why: CPALL has a clear parent-holder layer and a clear subsidiary layer from official sources.
The same node and edge model used for PTT can represent both incoming holders and downstream controlled entities.
The main caveat is that market intermediary nodes like Thai NVDR and custodian banks add noise, so the UI should support filtering.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show strategic owners first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
toggle direct ownership vs effective ownership

nodes.label: Charoen Pokphand Foods Public Company Limited
South East Asia UK (Type C) Nominees Limited
The Bank of New York Mellon
Nortrust Nominees Limited-NTC-RE IEDU UCITS 10 PCT

edges.notes: Direct holding reported on the CP Axtra major shareholders page.
CP ALL one report reports 59.93 percent effective ownership; CP Axtra shareholder page shows the split as 34.92 percent direct plus 25.01 percent via Siam Makro Holding.
Useful test case because shareholding and voting control differ.

why: PTT has a clean state-linked owner layer and a clearly visible downstream listed ecosystem.
The same node and edge schema can represent both direct holdings and multi-step group relationships through holding companies.
Compared with CPALL, the PTT graph benefits more from second-layer nodes because the value of the map comes from seeing listed subsidiaries linked to one another.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show direct ownership first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
allow an expand-to-second-layer toggle for holdings through Siam Management, PTTGC, TOP and OR

edges.notes: The PTTEP major shareholders page states that Siam Management Holding Company Limited is a wholly owned subsidiary of PTT.
This data point uses the SET listed company snapshot because the current official IR page still shows an older book-closing date.
Minority strategic holding rather than control, included to test whether the model can show adjacent ecosystem links as well as controlled subsidiaries.

graphs.focusLabel: Bangkok Expressway and Metro Public Company Limited

why: BEM has a clean strategic-owner layer and a compact operating-subsidiary layer from official sources.
Unlike the auto-generated context view, this map uses actual shareholder percentages and reported ownership stakes in core subsidiaries.
The main caveat is that BEM also appears in its own shareholder register through treasury shares, which is omitted here to avoid self-loop noise.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show strategic owners first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
keep BMN and NECL visible because they are the clearest downstream operating entities

nodes.label: Bangkok Expressway and Metro Public Company Limited
Mass Rapid Transit Authority of Thailand
Krung Thai Bank Public Company Limited
South East Asia UK (Type C) Nominees Limited

graphs.focusLabel: BTS Group Holdings Public Company Limited

why: BTS has a visible control layer at the top and a clear stack of listed and unlisted operating subsidiaries beneath it.
The annual report provides direct ownership percentages for the core rail and media entities, so this view is closer to a true control map than the old market-context fallback.
Some parts of the group use preferred-share structures, so this first version focuses on direct stakes that are explicitly stated in official materials.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show family-control and state-linked holders first
keep BTSC and VGI visible as the two primary downstream hubs
treat Rabbit and other complex preferred-share structures as later expansion layers

nodes.label: BTS Group Holdings Public Company Limited

graphs.focusLabel: Airports of Thailand Public Company Limited

why: AOT has a very clear state-enterprise ownership structure led by the Ministry of Finance.
Its one report also discloses the ownership percentages of core operating subsidiaries, so the graph can show both incoming holders and downstream operating entities with reported stakes.
Several downstream entities are held at 49 to 50 percent, so the map is useful for seeing how AOT mixes control, joint-control and strategic operating partnerships.
recommendedDefaultFilters: pin the Ministry of Finance node first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
keep AOTGA, AVSEC and SAH visible because they represent the clearest operating layer

edges.notes: AOT increased its AVSEC stake from 49 percent to 50 percent during FY2025 according to the one report note on subsidiary investments.

why: KTC is one of the simplest cases because the ownership story is dominated by a clear anchor shareholder and an otherwise straightforward institutional register.
This first dedicated map focuses on reported major shareholders only, which is enough to replace the old market-context fallback with a true shareholding view.
The downstream operating structure can be added later, but the incoming layer already captures the key control and float signals.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show anchor owner first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
treat this as an incoming-ownership-first map until subsidiary detail is added

graphs.focusLabel: Advanced Info Service Public Company Limited

why: ADVANC is also relatively simple because the official shareholder structure clearly identifies a dominant strategic owner plus a small set of institutional and nominee holders.
This dedicated map replaces the fallback with an explicit ownership view even before adding any downstream operating entities.
The key nuance is that part of the Singtel position is visible through a nominee account, so strategic and nominee layers should be interpreted together.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show Gulf and Singtel first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
treat this as a shareholder-register map until subsidiary detail is added

why: DELTA is straightforward to add because the official shareholding page clearly discloses the control cluster around Delta group entities.
A dedicated ownership map is valuable here because the top register is unusually concentrated and tells a more meaningful story than sector or RRG neighbors.
This first version focuses on incoming ownership and leaves operating subsidiaries for a later pass.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show the Delta group entities first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
treat this as a concentrated-control map until subsidiary detail is added

nodes.label: UBS AG Hong Kong Branch - For Client's Accounts

why: KBANK is a good low-complexity addition because the shareholder structure page already provides a clean top-holder register on a single official page.
The map shows one important cross-link to another SET50 company through Gulf, while the rest of the incoming layer is mostly institutional and nominee capital.
This makes KBANK a useful comparison case against KTC and ADVANC without needing downstream subsidiary detail yet.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show Gulf first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default
treat this as an incoming-shareholder map until subsidiary detail is added

why: BBL is another simple incoming-ownership case because the investor-relations page publishes a compact top-10 register with a fresh April 2026 record date.

A small listed-company cross-link appears through Bangkok Insurance, while the rest can remain as non-listed holder nodes for now.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show listed and strategic holders first

This is a good benchmark for state-linked or royal-linked concentration without needing second-layer subsidiary edges yet.

graphs.focusLabel: TISCO Financial Group Public Company Limited

The map is useful because it combines one dominant foreign strategic block, one Thai listed-company cross-holding through TCAP, and a typical custody layer.
This gives the relations view another financial-sector example without needing any subsidiary expansion yet.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show CDIB, Tokyo Century and TCAP first
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default

why: TTB is worth adding early because the official major-shareholders page already exposes the core control blocks clearly even without a full top-10 table.
This first-pass map focuses on the four dominant reported holders, which already explains most of the bank's ownership structure.
The model can be expanded later with the rest of the float and nominee layer, but the anchor ownership story is already visible.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show anchor holders first
treat this as a concentrated-shareholder map

graphs.focusLabel: Kiatnakin Phatra Bank Public Company Limited

why: KKP is another clean incoming-ownership case because the investor-relations page provides a full top-10 table with a fresh April 2026 record date.
The map is useful because it mixes one listed-company cross-holding through RAM, one self-holding line, and several family or private-company anchors.
This makes KKP a richer financial-sector example than a pure nominee-driven register.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show private-company anchors first
keep the self-holding line visible but de-emphasized
hide nominee and custodian nodes by default

graphs.focusLabel: Charoen Pokphand Foods Public Company Limited

why: CPF works well in the model because the ownership story is anchored by a very visible CP group cluster rather than by one isolated holder.
That makes it a good ecosystem map where related holding companies, nominee accounts, and state-linked funds all appear around the same focus company.
The source also shows CPF holding its own shares, but that self-holding line is omitted here to avoid self-loop clutter in the first-pass graph.
recommendedDefaultFilters: show CP group strategic holders first
dim nominee and custodian nodes by default
treat this as a group-control map until cross-company CP links are expanded
